logistics.explanation: Gangwon Province is accessible from Seoul via KTX high-speed rail in 2 hours. Local cycling infrastructure is developing.

length.explanation: The 150km route offers a substantial day in the saddle along the Gangwon coastline and mountain roads.

technicality.explanation: Well-maintained Korean roads with good surface quality. Standard road sportive demands.

elevation.explanation: Roughly 2100m of climbing through the coastal mountains of eastern Korea.

climate.explanation: October brings cool autumn temperatures around 10-18C. Occasional coastal winds.

altitude.explanation: Maximum elevation around 700m. No significant altitude concerns.

adventure.explanation: Scenic coastal roads and mountain passes through Gangwon Province offer varied terrain.

prestige.explanation: Growing event in Korea's cycling scene but still building international recognition.

race_quality.explanation: Organized by local cycling federation with aid stations and mechanical support.

experience.explanation: Beautiful autumn foliage along Korea's eastern coast and mountain roads.

community.explanation: Strong local cycling community participation. Growing Korean amateur cycling scene.

field_depth.explanation: Around 1500 participants, primarily Korean domestic riders with some international visitors.

value.explanation: Entry fees around 50000-80000 KRW (roughly $40-60 USD). Reasonable for the experience.

expenses.explanation: Moderate costs. Accommodation in Yangyang is affordable. Seoul provides cheaper base options.

cultural_impact.explanation: Part of Korea's growing cycling culture. Yangyang is known for surfing and outdoor sports.

course_description.summary: The Yangyang Gran Fondo traces the dramatic eastern coastline of Gangwon Province before turning inland through forested mountain passes. The route showcases autumn colors and Pacific Ocean views.

key_segments.name: Coastal Highway
key_segments.description: Opening section along Korea's eastern seaboard with ocean panoramas.

key_segments.name: Osaek Mountain Pass
key_segments.description: The main climb through pine forests with autumn foliage.

course_description.elevation_profile: Generally rolling with two significant climbs in the second half. The route gains most elevation between miles 40-70.
course_description.aid_stations: Aid stations every 25-30km with water, electrolytes, and Korean snacks.

travel.driving_from_major_city: 2.5 hours from Seoul via Yeongdong Expressway
travel.accommodation: Hotels and pensions in Yangyang beach area. Budget options from 50000 KRW/night.
travel.bike_transport: Bike bags accepted on KTX trains. Local rental shops available in Yangyang.

training_recommendations.minimum_fitness: Able to ride 100km with moderate climbing. 6+ weeks of structured training.
training_recommendations.ideal_preparation: 12 weeks of endurance-focused training including hill repeats and long rides of 120km+.
training_recommendations.key_workouts: Weekly hill intervals, tempo rides on rolling terrain, back-to-back weekend rides.

history.notable_facts: Part of the Korean cycling boom following increased infrastructure investment. Gangwon Province hosted cycling events during the 2018 PyeongChang Winter Olympics period.

final_verdict.one_liner: A scenic Korean coastal-to-mountain gran fondo showcasing Gangwon Province's autumn beauty.
final_verdict.who_should_ride: Riders seeking an authentic Korean cycling experience with coastal and mountain scenery.
final_verdict.who_should_skip: Those expecting a large international field or flat fast course.
final_verdict.insider_tip: Stay in Yangyang's beach district and visit the Naksansa Temple the day before the ride.

suffering_zones.label: Osaek Ascent
suffering_zones.description: The main climb gains 500m over 15km through dense forest. Steady gradient with limited shade breaks.

suffering_zones.label: Coastal Headwinds
suffering_zones.description: The return coastal section often faces strong onshore winds in the afternoon.
